name: Kalk Bay Harbour
description: Working fishing harbour on the False Bay coast. Pier and harbour wall access, good for hottentot, galjoen and the occasional yellowtail. Family-friendly and easy shore access.
permit: A recreational saltwater fishing permit (post office) is required.

name: Miller's Point
description: Classic Cape rock-angling venue south of Simon's Town. Deep water close in — targets include kob, yellowtail and geelbek. Watch the swell; not a spot for novices on a pushing tide.
permit: Recreational saltwater permit required. Falls within the Table Mountain National Park MPA — check current zoning.

name: Simon's Town Jetty
description: Sheltered jetty fishing inside Simon's Town naval harbour area. Hottentot, steentjies and panga from the wall. Good winter option when the rocks are blown out.
permit: Recreational saltwater permit required.

name: Strand Beach
description: Long sandy surf beach on the eastern side of False Bay. Kob, elf (shad) and white steenbras are the main targets. Best fished early morning or late afternoon on a pushing tide.
permit: Recreational saltwater permit required. Observe seasonal closures for shad (elf).

name: Muizenberg Beach
description: Gentle, wide beach — ideal for beginners learning to cast a surf rod. Shad, kob, and occasional galjoen on a running tide. Shark spotters operate here; heed the flags.
permit: Recreational saltwater permit required.

name: Cape Point
description: Serious ledge fishing at the tip of the Cape Peninsula. Big yellowtail and kob are possible but the approach is long, the ledges exposed, and the swell unforgiving. For experienced anglers only.
permit: Park conservation fee plus recreational saltwater permit. Much of the area is within the MPA — check zoning before fishing.

name: Hout Bay Harbour
description: Harbour wall and breakwater access on the Atlantic side. Hottentot, mackerel and the occasional snoek from the wall during winter runs.
permit: Recreational saltwater permit required.

name: Milnerton Lagoon
description: Diep River mouth and lagoon system. Light tackle for white steenbras, leervis (garrick) and grunter. Best on an incoming tide, working lures or live prawn.
permit: Recreational saltwater permit required. Check current local regulations on bait collection.

name: Kommetjie — Long Beach
description: Long sandy stretch running up toward Noordhoek, with rocky headlands at the Kom itself. Kob and shad on the beach, hottentot and galjoen from the rocks. Exposed to Atlantic swell.
permit: Recreational saltwater permit required.

name: Gordon's Bay Harbour Wall
description: Sheltered harbour wall fishing at the eastern end of False Bay. Hottentot, steentjies and panga are the regulars. Good winter spot when the south-easter is blowing.
permit: Recreational saltwater permit required.

name: Bloubergstrand
description: Iconic Atlantic surf beach with the Table Mountain view. White steenbras, kob and elf along the trough. Often blown clean by the south-easter, so pick your day.
permit: Recreational saltwater permit required.

name: Melkbosstrand
description: Long, gently sloping Atlantic beach north of Cape Town. Reliable for kob and white steenbras at dawn and dusk; lighter tackle works for galjoen along the rockier sections.
permit: Recreational saltwater permit required.

name: Yzerfontein — Schaapen Island side
description: West Coast village with quality rock and surf fishing on either side of the slipway. Galjoen on the rocks in winter, kob and white steenbras on the beach. Watch your footing on the kelp-covered ledges.
permit: Recreational saltwater permit required. Galjoen has a strict closed season — check current dates.

name: Langebaan Lagoon
description: Sheltered lagoon ideal for light tackle. Grunter, white steenbras and the occasional leervis. Most of the lagoon is inside the West Coast National Park MPA — fishing is restricted to designated zones only.
permit: Park entry fee plus recreational saltwater permit. Strict MPA zoning — check West Coast NP rules before fishing.

name: Pringle Bay
description: Quiet bay south-east of Gordon's Bay with mixed rock and sand. Galjoen, hottentot and the occasional musselcracker from the rocks; kob and elf along the beach.
permit: Recreational saltwater permit required.

name: Rooi-Els
description: Steep, deep ledges along the cliffs of Rooi-Els — a serious rock spot. Yellowtail, geelbek and kob are the prizes; the swell and rugged access keep all but experienced anglers away.
permit: Recreational saltwater permit required. Always fish with a partner — exposed and unforgiving.

name: Hermanus — New Harbour
description: Working harbour east of the Cape with reliable wall fishing. Hottentot, panga and steentjies year-round; baby kob and snoek inside the harbour during their runs. Easy access for families.
permit: Recreational saltwater permit required. Whale season (Jun–Nov) draws crowds — arrive early.

name: Witsand — Breede River Mouth
description: Breede River mouth — South Africa's most famous kob estuary. Big dusky kob on live mullet, plus garrick on lures. Boat or wading angling; most of the estuary has strict bag and size limits.
permit: Recreational saltwater permit required. Check current Breede River kob slot limits before fishing.

name: Cape Agulhas
description: Africa's southernmost tip — broad, low-lying rock platforms dotted with gullies. Galjoen, hottentot and white musselcracker. Wind-blown most days; pick a calm window.
permit: Recreational saltwater permit required. Inside the Agulhas National Park — check fishing zones.

name: Stilbaai — Goukou River
description: Sheltered Goukou River estuary on the Garden Route side. Light-tackle estuary fishing for grunter, white steenbras and leervis. Great family destination with plenty of bank access.
permit: Recreational saltwater permit required. Catch-and-release is encouraged — the system is small and pressured.
